# Taxonomy Alignment

- [ ] Preserves the stable 14 top-level buckets.
- [ ] Maps each artifact to one canonical bucket.
- [ ] Treats protocol or framework surfaces as mappings or adapters, not taxonomy replacements.

# Memory And State Boundary

- [ ] Keeps memory policy concerns separate from state strategy concerns.
- [ ] Does not include real memory entries, live memory exports, session snapshots, or runtime state.

# Design-Time, Runtime, And Iteration Boundary

- [ ] Keeps intended behavior, runtime assumptions, and iteration notes distinct.
- [ ] Does not collapse policies, prompts, schemas, runtime configs, evals, or changelog notes into one artifact.

# Public-Safety Review

- [ ] Uses synthetic, generic examples only.
- [ ] Includes no secrets, credentials, account IDs, private URLs, private data, regulated data, proprietary workflows, logs, traces, memory entries, or live state snapshots.
- [ ] Public-safety notes were added or preserved where relevant.
